global.name: Findahelpline
global.label: Find a free, verified helpline in your country
notes: phone, text, and chat options may vary by country

UK.name: NHS urgent mental health help
UK.label: Get urgent NHS mental health help
notes: use NHS 111 or urgent mental health support

professionalCareOptions.title: Public / national mental health services
professionalCareOptions.description: Use national or public urgent mental health services first where they are available.

professionalCareOptions.title: Primary care / doctor / GP
professionalCareOptions.description: Contact a doctor or GP for urgent same-day mental health support and referral.

professionalCareOptions.title: Licensed therapist or psychiatrist
professionalCareOptions.description: Arrange urgent review with a licensed therapist, psychologist, or psychiatrist.

professionalCareOptions.title: Private urgent mental health clinic
professionalCareOptions.description: Use a private urgent mental health clinic if that is the fastest available option.

nextStepsChecklist: Contact a crisis line or urgent mental health service now
Reach out to a trusted person to stay with you or support you
Go to emergency care if you may act on thoughts of self-harm or feel unsafe
Return to the app later once immediate support is in place
